checkpoint: uncommitted script-test owner cutover with the active feature still in progress
scope: Script-test and fixture ownership under scripts/tests
observed_implementation: All 95 formerly flat files under scripts/tests moved under checks, dev, harness, lib, platform, or release directories that mirror their production script owners.
The recursive script-test runner still discovers every nested test deterministically.
The repository topology contract rejects flat script-test support files and undeclared script-test owner directories.
Path-sensitive release, external AI, security sensor, CODEOWNERS fixture, documentation, and file-size policy consumers use the owner-mirrored paths.
Existing no-growth file-size exception ceilings were preserved without increases.
The live source-boundary sensor now recognizes the current desktop bootstrap and IPC request-ledger registrations instead of retired paths.

verification.result: The new owner-mirroring regression failed before implementation because flat and arbitrary script-test paths were accepted.
All 11 topology tests passed after the owner-mirroring rule was added.
All 24 focused release packaging and public artifact tests passed after repository-root resolution was updated for the nested owner.
Every governed file remained within its existing hard limit or exact no-growth exception.
All 817 script and harness tests passed in the owner-mirrored layout.

verification.command: npm run harness:check && npm run lint:architecture && npm run lint:tests
verification.result: Canonical state, file coverage, repository topology, frontend boundaries, and test-quality contracts passed.

verification.result: Repository residue, the 778-line duplication baseline, and secret scanning passed after the stale desktop registration sensor was corrected.
Markdown, prose, state documentation, and patch whitespace checks passed.
